title: DVWA (Damn Vulnerable Web Application)
html:
DVWA (Damn Vulnerable Web Application)

Intentionally insecure PHP/MySQL web app used in OSU coursework for hands-on web-vulnerability practice. Yahir's June 1, 2025 lab-burst day is preserved across 10 timestamped screenshots — the densest single-day study record in the corpus. Sister tool to Wireshark in the summer 2025 OSU security capstone era.

The June 1, 2025 single-day burst

All ten screenshots from one continuous afternoon → evening session — DVWA running under XAMPP on Yahir's M1 Mac:

  • 2025 06 01 screenshot 2025 06 01 at 11 59 16 am f28e59b9 — 11:59am, kicking off
  • 2025 06 01 screenshot 2025 06 01 at 12 42 16 pm 0a85539e — 12:42pm
  • 2025 06 01 screenshot 2025 06 01 at 12 50 51 pm ca01af2a — 12:50pm
  • 2025 06 01 screenshot 2025 06 01 at 1 05 02 pm 386b66ab — 1:05pm
  • 2025 06 01 screenshot 2025 06 01 at 1 09 56 pm e96e6553 — 1:09pm
  • 2025 06 01 screenshot 2025 06 01 at 1 10 01 pm edb0daf3 — 1:10pm
  • 2025 06 01 screenshot 2025 06 01 at 1 10 08 pm 8ee70f58 — 1:10pm (screenshot-spam moment)
  • 2025 06 01 screenshot 2025 06 01 at 1 13 22 pm 2eb30891 — 1:13pm
  • 2025 06 01 screenshot 2025 06 01 at 1 16 30 pm 173fbfb8 — 1:16pm
  • 2025 06 01 screenshot 2025 06 01 at 1 20 30 pm 217289fd — 1:20pm — wraps the burst

Vulnerability classes covered

XSS (DOM/Reflected/Stored), SQL Injection, CSRF, File Inclusion, File Upload, Brute Force, CAPTCHA bypass, Weak Session IDs, JavaScript flaws, Authorisation bypass.

Why it matters

DVWA is the canonical hands-on artifact of the network forensics / malware analysis lab coursework cluster. Eight days later (June 9-11) Yahir runs the parallel Wireshark burst on a separate VM — same shape, different layer (network vs web-app). Both are him converting CS-coursework theory into hands-on motor skill the week he's about to graduate out of OSU.

Connections

wireshark · network forensics · mobile malware · reverse engineering · malware analysis lab · surveillance state · xampp · m1 mac · cs coursework · osu
